title: Hawley Watch
description: Political accountability site tracking Senator Josh Hawley's public statements against his voting record and campaign finance data. Fair, sourced, and transparent.

detail.tagline: A political-accountability tracker measuring what Senator Josh Hawley says against how he actually votes.
detail.role: Solo — research, data, scoring system, and site.
overview: Politicians say one thing and vote another, and the gap is hard to see without doing the homework. Hawley Watch does that homework for one U.S. senator: it pairs public statements with the legislative record, scores each claim, and connects the money and the votes to what Missourians actually experience.

Every number traces to a public source — GovTrack and Congress.gov for votes, OpenSecrets and FEC filings for money, BLS and census data for local impact. The site is deliberately even-handed: a 'Credit Where It's Due' section documents where his words and record align, because credibility comes from fairness.

gallery.caption: Home — the accountability dashboard: consistency ratio, average truth score, career fundraising, and the latest scored analysis.
Voting Record — key votes flagged Consistent or Contradiction against his public rhetoric, each with a sourced note.
Money Trail — career and cycle fundraising by industry, drawn from OpenSecrets and FEC filings.
Impact — cost-of-living, housing, healthcare and democracy metrics from official data, each tied back to a vote.
Credit Where It's Due — the other side of the ledger: positions where his statements and record genuinely align.

features.title: Truth-scored analysis
features.detail: Each claim is fact-checked and scored 0–100 with a plain-language verdict, rolled into a running average truth score.

features.title: Says-vs-does voting record
features.detail: A sortable table of key votes, each flagged Consistent or Contradiction against his rhetoric, with a note explaining the call.

features.title: Follow the money
features.detail: Career and cycle fundraising broken down by industry from OpenSecrets and FEC data, surfacing conflicts between donors and positions.

features.title: Impact on Missouri
features.detail: Cost-of-living, housing, healthcare and democracy metrics from official sources, each tied back to a specific vote.

features.title: Credit where it's due
features.detail: The other side of the ledger — documented positions where his statements and votes genuinely align.

challenges.title: One accountability score across very different pages
challenges.problem: Fact-check posts, a votes table, donor breakdowns, and impact metrics look nothing alike, yet all need to feed one consistency ratio and truth score that never drifts from the underlying entries.
challenges.outcome: Everything is authored as typed Astro content collections and structured JSON; a single computeStats routine derives the headline numbers across posts, votes, and the tweet archive, so the dashboard always reconciles with its sources.

challenges.title: Sourced, not asserted
challenges.problem: An accountability site is worthless if readers can't check it — every claim needs a traceable source, not just a verdict.
challenges.outcome: Each vote, dollar figure, and impact metric carries its source (GovTrack, Congress.gov, OpenSecrets, FEC, BLS) inline, and the methodology is documented so any figure can be independently verified.

challenges.title: Even-handed by construction
challenges.problem: A partisan-looking accountability tool gets dismissed by exactly the audience it most needs to reach.
challenges.outcome: Claims are bucketed as consistent, receipt, or noise by the same rules regardless of who they flatter, and a dedicated 'Credit Where It's Due' feed surfaces the consistent ones — fairness is encoded in the data model, not just the tone.

stack.name: Astro
stack.why: Static multi-page site — fast, cacheable, no backend to run.

stack.name: Content collections
stack.why: Typed fact-check posts and credit entries with schema validation.

stack.name: TypeScript
stack.why: A single stats module derives the consistency ratio and truth score.

stack.name: Structured JSON
stack.why: Votes, donor money, and impact metrics as sourced, verifiable records.
